topic: Boundary with Chapter 8: Transferred Representations vs. Internal Compression
scope: Clarifying the boundary: Ch 8 is compressing your own data without supervision; Ch 15 is transferring semantic representations from massive external data.
key_concepts: There is a distinct conceptual boundary between dimensionality reduction (covered in Chapter 8) and the pretrained embeddings discussed here.
Methods in Chapter 8, such as PCA or autoencoders, compress the specific dataset you currently possess without injecting any outside knowledge.
Pretrained models transfer rich semantic representations learned from massive, external datasets into your pipeline.
An autoencoder trained from scratch on your own data is an internal compression technique, despite using a neural network.
The true dividing line is not 'statistical methods versus neural networks', but rather 'compressing your own data versus transferring external knowledge'.
examples: Training an autoencoder to reduce 1,000 tabular features down to 50 dimensions is internal data compression. In contrast, passing text through a pretrained BERT model to get 768-dimensional vectors is transferring external representations.

relations.precedes: ch15_s05 (the debate on whether deep learning replaces feature engineering entirely).
relations.follows: ch15_s03 (domain shift, which is specific to external data transfer).

exclusions: Do not re-explain the mechanics of PCA, SVD, or Autoencoders.
